Access Property ServicesVisual Building Inspection · V2
Visual Building Inspection · AS 4349.1 (general spirit)

152 Moverly Road, South Coogee — Top (Northern) Townhouse

This report sets out the findings of a visual building inspection conducted on site by Dominic Ogburn on 23 April 2026. The inspection was supplemented by a photographic review of 244 images. Each finding below is accompanied by photographic evidence.

InspectorDominic Ogburn
Inspection date23 April 2026
Subject dwellingTop (northern) townhouse — pool side
Lot / PlanLot 4784, DP 752015
Photographs reviewed244 images · 41 zones
Report versionV2
Note: Prepared in the general spirit of AS 4349.1:2007. Specialist scopes (pool, roof, fire safety, NATA water test) are referenced where engaged. See Appendix B for scope disclaimers.
Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 1
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Summary

Summary & Major Findings

Forty-four discrete findings were recorded across the basement, ground floor, upper level and roof / exterior. Severity is assigned per finding — major items represent confirmed life-safety or NCC-compliance issues and must be resolved before pool use, cooktop use or occupancy.

44Total findings
3Major
14Moderate
16Minor
9Verify

Major findings — priority actions

3 confirmed life-safety / NCC-compliance items. Resolve before pool use, cooktop use or occupancy.

⚠ Important — B7: Fire separation between basement (Class 10a) and dwelling (Class 1a) — absent on site
⚠ Important — G12: Fire separation to basement parking — Ground Floor side cross-reference of B7
⚠ Important — G3: Glass pool barrier panels (pool-side) — excessive lateral flex
Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 2
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Basement
B

Basement

The basement parking is the most compliance-sensitive zone in this dwelling. Items below cover fire-resistance separation between the basement (Class 10a) and the dwelling (Class 1a), structural concrete observations, the retaining-wall membrane termination, and door / hardware finishing.

8 findings · 1 Major · 4 Moderate · 3 Minor
B7
Fire separation between basement (Class 10a) and dwelling (Class 1a) — absent on site
MAJOR
Primary photo: P1140576
Location
Basement — basement-to-dwelling transitions
Group
Fire safety
Photo references
p1140576, p1140582, p1140580, p1140581, p1140671
Observation

There is no fire-resistant separating wall installed between the basement parking and the Ground Floor dwelling. No FRL 60/60/60 wall, no masonry alternative, no tight-fitting self-closing solid-core door at any of the transitions (stair head, lift landing or any other doorway) — confirmed against Basement Plan CDC 3.00 May 2023. The basement and the Ground Floor are effectively open to each other via the spiral stair and lift shaft. The internal spiral stair has no handrail. Related items in the same room: an unmapped fully-enclosed storage space at the rear driveway (not on CDC 3.00) and a disconnected A/C diffuser inside it.

Recommendation

Obtain written confirmation from a registered Fire Safety Engineer or registered building certifier that the dwelling does not comply. Raise with the builder under statutory warranty (Home Building Act 1989 NSW). Rectification scope: FRL 60/60/60 separating construction at every basement-to-Class-1a transition; tight-fitting self-closing solid-core doors (≥ 35 mm); AS 1735.11 fire-rated lift landing doors; install a continuous handrail to the spiral stair (NCC HP 11.2). Interim: do not store fuel, LPG cylinders or lithium-ion batteries in the basement; install interconnected smoke alarms; install a CO monitor at the stair head.

Australian Standards engaged
  • NCC 2022 HP H6D1 — separating wall / floor FRL 60/60/60
  • NCC 2022 HP H6D2 — door requirements in separating wall
  • NCC 2022 HP 11.2 — internal stair handrail
  • AS 1530.4:2014 — Fire-resistance tests
  • AS 1905.1:2015 — Fire-resistant doorsets
  • AS 1735.11:1986 — Fire-rated lift landing doors
B2
Historic moisture staining in service / bonding riser
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140559
Location
Basement — service / bonding riser
Group
Service riser
Photo references
p1140559
Observation

Historic moisture staining / efflorescence pattern visible on galvanised panel and adjacent pipe; streaked staining down the pipe is consistent with prior water tracking. No active water observed.

Recommendation

Investigate source (above-slab penetrations, waterproofing at service chase, pipe joint integrity). Confirm electrical equipment is rated for the exposure it has experienced; verify the integrity of equipotential bonding (licensed electrician scope).

B3A
Drummy (delaminated) acrylic render at basement driveway entry & planter
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140786
Location
Basement — driveway entry & planter
Group
Render
Photo references
p1140786, p1140788, p1140789
Observation

Sound-testing indicates the majority of the acrylic render to the eastern driveway entry wall below the entry steps is substantially drummy — the render has lost adhesion to the substrate. Same condition across the horizontal top of the rendered planter wall along the eastern entry footpath. Entry-step tiling shows open joints / gaps at the curved wall.

Recommendation

Remove all drummy render back to a sound edge, key the substrate, re-render per the original specification and repaint. Rectify all tile joints / gaps at the adjacent entry-step tiling and curved wall.

B4
Crack in polished concrete floor slab (basement parking)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140571
Location
Basement — basement parking
Group
Concrete
Photo references
p1140571
Observation

A visible crack runs across the polished slab; width cannot be measured from the photograph but appears > 0.3 mm. Approximately linear — consistent with an uncontrolled drying-shrinkage crack or a crack at an inadequate control joint.

Recommendation

Measure crack width with a crack-width gauge; install a tell-tale monitor and observe for 3–6 months. If > 0.3 mm and stable, consider epoxy injection. If active or > 1.0 mm, engage a structural engineer.

B5
Rust through external door jamb; render crack; poor gyprock finishing; under-stair door leaf not installed
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140575
Location
Basement — under-stair
Group
Render
Photo references
p1140575, p1140579, p1140789
Observation

Rust-coloured staining bleeding through the painted door jamb finish, consistent with corrosion of embedded steel, fixings or a steel lintel behind the paint. Crack / gap visible at the render-to-jamb interface. Adjacent lower gyprock wall shows very poor finishing — joint misalignment, uneven setting compound, visible fastener heads. The under-stair door leaf has not been installed — opening currently unsecured.

Recommendation

Strip back paint at the rusting area; identify and remediate the corroding element; apply a paint system per AS/NZS 2312.1 for the exposure class. Re-seal the render / jamb junction. Make good adjacent gyprock to a Level 4 finish per AS 2589.1. Install the under-stair door leaf with a compliant weather seal.

B1
Basement retaining wall — remove redundant formwork; make good exposed membrane termination & honeycombed concrete (reclassified V1 Major → V2 Minor)
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140558
Location
Basement — retaining wall
Group
Concrete
Photo references
p1140558
Observation

Redundant formwork in place. Exposed black waterproofing membrane / sheet visible with unrendered concrete surround. Surrounding concrete shows honeycombing. Soil / backfill visible against the unfinished concrete; the protective cover to the membrane termination is absent.

Recommendation

Refer to the building's consulting engineer. Remove redundant formwork. Make good honeycombed concrete; reinstate protective cover / flashing to the membrane termination per the manufacturer's installation details. Verify membrane continuity. Photograph during rectification.

B3
Excess concrete around drainage grate; delaminated slab paving adjacent
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140563
Location
Basement — external drainage grate
Group
Drainage
Photo references
p1140563
Observation

Excess concrete spill around the drainage grate and delaminated / spalling slab paving adjacent to the grate. Organic debris accumulated in the pit.

Recommendation

Chip back the excess concrete and make good the damaged slab paving; confirm the grate is securely retained and of the appropriate class; schedule regular cleaning.

B6
Corrosion staining on garage-door operating spring
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140567
Location
Basement — garage door
Group
Garage door
Photo references
p1140567, p1140568
Observation

The garage-door operating spring shows significant rust-coloured corrosion staining along its length. Progressive corrosion will weaken the spring and lead to premature failure — a failed torsion spring can drop a heavy door (safety risk).

Recommendation

Engage a licensed garage-door technician to inspect the spring, fixings and cable assembly; replace corroded components with coastal-rated (C5-M) equivalents; review the opener balance after replacement.

Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 3
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Ground Floor
G

Ground Floor

Ground-floor findings cover the pool barrier and pool-side glazing, the entertaining deck and its concealed water-catchment tray, weatherproofing of external door / window junctions, and a number of internal finish observations.

14 findings · 2 Major · 5 Moderate · 2 Minor · 4 Verify · 1 Observation
G12
Fire separation to basement parking — Ground Floor side cross-reference of B7
MAJOR
Primary photo: P1140576
Location
Ground Floor — basement parking
Group
Fire safety
Photo references
(see B7 photos)
Observation

Ground Floor side cross-reference of the same finding recorded under B7. The required FRL 60/60/60 separation, masonry alternative, and tight-fitting self-closing solid-core doors are all absent. The basement and the Ground Floor are effectively open to each other via the spiral stair and lift shaft.

Recommendation

See B7 for the full rectification scope. Rectification must cover the Ground Floor side of the separation (floor slab, stair-head enclosure, lift landing door) in addition to the basement side.

Australian Standards engaged
  • See B7
G3
Glass pool barrier panels (pool-side) — excessive lateral flex
MAJOR
Primary photo: P1140688
Location
Ground Floor — pool fence (pool side)
Group
Pool barrier
Photo references
p1140688, p1140696
Observation

The glass pool barrier panels are fixed to the deck via small base spigots (brand: Minrell) with no continuous top rail on the pool-side panels. Under manual load applied to the panel face the panels show significant lateral flex. This is inconsistent with AS 1288 retention requirements — monolithic toughened glass panels fixed by point fixings / spigots generally require a continuous top rail unless a recognised non-top-rail system is installed and certified under the AGGA E1 scheme. Recommended specialist contact for rectification / re-inspection: Phil — 0434 035 119 (E1 Certifier).

Recommendation

Replace with an E1-certified base-mounted glass balustrade / pool-fence system. Verify Grade A safety glass marking, panel thickness/height/spigot spacing per the manufacturer's certified tables; barrier height ≥ 1200 mm above the outside finished surface; gap from bottom of panel to deck ≤ 100 mm; gap between adjacent panels ≤ 100 mm; non-climbable zone (NCZ) clear within 900 mm of the outside. Obtain a Certificate of Compliance from an accredited NSW Pool Barrier Certifier and re-register on the NSW Swimming Pool Register before pool use.

Australian Standards engaged
  • AS 1926.1:2012 — Pool-safety barriers
  • AS 1288:2021 Section 7 — Glass balustrades
  • AS/NZS 2208:1996 — Grade A safety glazing
  • AS 1170.1:2002 — Barrier design loads
  • Swimming Pools Act 1992 (NSW); Swimming Pools Regulation 2018 (NSW)
G1
Cracking & delamination of protective paint coating on balcony steel support post (external)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140641
Location
Ground Floor — external balcony post
Group
Protective coatings
Photo references
p1140641, p1140642
Observation

Extensive cracking and delamination of the protective paint coating on the external balcony steel support post. Coating breakdown at this element exposes the steel to corrosion in a coastal / chlorinated-pool exposure environment. (Re-scoped in V2 from external-render map cracking.)

Recommendation

Engage a specialist coatings contractor to strip back the existing paint system on the affected post, prepare the substrate (abrasive blast or power-tool clean to AS 1627 appropriate class), treat any corrosion, and apply a new paint system certified to AS/NZS 2312.1 for the exposure class (C5-M for this coastal site). Retain the contractor's warranty.

G10
Entertaining deck — concealed water-catchment tray with no access and no emergency overflow
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140681
Location
Ground Floor — entertaining deck
Group
Drainage & overflow
Photo references
p1140681, p1140689, p1140691, p1140696, p1140697
Observation

Composite timber decking on a deck frame system. Board spacing and fixings appear irregular and there is evidence of Cypress tree debris between board gaps. The South-facing L-shaped entertaining area sits over a metal waterproof water-catchment tray beneath the decking. No access trap / panel to the concealed drain(s) for clearing or maintenance, and no evidence of an emergency overflow. The deck sits flush with the Living Room door sill — vulnerable to wind-driven rain ingress.

Recommendation

Install access panels / removable deck sections over all concealed drains. Install a compliant emergency overflow outlet to the water-catchment tray, discharging to a visible external point. Confirm deck framing/fixings by reference to construction records. Pendulum slip test (AS 4586) in wet conditions — apply anti-slip treatment if below P3.

G12A
Floating timber floor (G12A) — no expansion provision; NW external entry door (G12B) admits wind-driven rain & scrapes on flooring (Major)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140724
Location
Ground Floor — Living/Kitchen — floating floor
Group
External doors
Photo references
p1140724, p1140738, p1140748, p1140753, p1140757, p1140758, p1140759, p1140761, p1140774
Observation

G12A — Floating timber floor: sealant-filled to perimeter skirtings throughout the entire Ground Floor, preventing free expansion / contraction. Living Room southern / eastern perimeter abuts a U-shaped angle with butt-fixed flooring and no expansion provision. G12B — NW external entry door (Major): entry door and sill abut the floating timber floor with no expansion joint; multiple paint and frame-finish damage; door fully exposed to prevailing NW weather, would permit easy entry of wind-driven rainwater. The bottom of the door leaf scrapes on the floating flooring when swinging, causing progressive abrasion damage at the entry arc. Other items: none of the internal doors incorporate rubber buffers — they impact heavily on jambs; very few cupboard / joinery doors incorporate rubber buffers; the kitchen island bench underside is not sealed to the flooring (vermin / dust path).

Recommendation

Carefully remove all sealant filling at skirtings and abutting joinery to restore floating-floor expansion. At the Living Room U-angle, create a ~5 mm gap and fix a colour-matched plastic micro-bead glue-fixed to the sill (not the flooring). Rectify the NW entry door (G12B): install a compliant weather threshold / drip-flashing / storm seal set; make good paintwork; install a proper expansion junction; consider an overhead weather hood; re-hang the door with adequate clearance (~8 mm) above the finished flooring; make good damaged floorboards. Install rubber buffers on all internal door jambs and cupboard / joinery doors. Seal the kitchen island bench underside to the floor.

G2
Damaged & excess render / paint at external window / door frame jamb
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140636
Location
Ground Floor — external window/door frame
Group
Render & paint
Photo references
p1140636, p1140637
Observation

Render is chipped and broken away at the base of the frame jamb, exposing the substrate, with excess render / paint build-up around the jamb edge. Junction between render and frame is not finished cleanly.

Recommendation

Cut back excess render / paint, make good the damaged render, and apply a neutral-cure silicone flexible sealant at the frame-to-render junction; verify the frame flashing / weather lip behind the render is continuous.

G7
Unfinished render / paint & exposed cabling near under-entry-path external A/C unit (separate unit from R8)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140698
Location
Ground Floor — entry-path A/C unit
Group
Render & paint
Photo references
p1140698
Observation

Render / paint at the base of the wall adjacent to the external A/C condenser is not made good — substrate visible in several places. Coloured wires / cables are visible protruding from a horizontal construction joint in the render at approximately 400 mm AFFL.

Recommendation

Engage the builder to make good the render / paint around the penetration, seal around the cabling with UV-stable flexible sealant, and verify exposed cables are adequately protected and terminated in a weather-rated enclosure per AS/NZS 3000. Verify A/C condenser clearances per the manufacturer's installation manual.

G6
Pool waterline — surface staining and floating debris (maintenance)
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140701
Location
Ground Floor — pool waterline
Group
Pool maintenance
Photo references
p1140701, p1140688
Observation

Brown / yellow staining visible at the waterline mosaic tile. Floating organic debris in the water. Water clarity slightly reduced.

Recommendation

Commission the pool (clean, balance chemistry, confirm filtration cycle). Provide handover documentation.

G8
Spiral stair soffit / ceiling finish — incomplete / touch-up visible
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140739
Location
Ground Floor — spiral stair
Group
Stairs & handrails
Photo references
p1140739, p1140740, p1140741
Observation

Underside / soffit along the stair void shows areas of partially-painted setting compound, a pale-green paint blob or primer patch, and a visible junction line between two surfaces not finished flush.

Recommendation

Make good plasterboard and paint to a Level 4 finish at the stair soffit per AS 2589.1.

G13

Verifications

Observation

The following Ground Floor items could not be verified from the photographs supplied and require further inspection: waterproofing to GF Bathroom & Guest Ensuite (AS 3740 certificate); mechanical ventilation from Bathroom, Guest Ensuite and Laundry; smoke alarm locations & interconnection on this level (AS 3786); Laundry tap / waste / drain / WM recess; entry-door compliance (weather seal, hardware); window-safety devices for GF; termite-management certificate (AS 3660.1); lift compliance (AS 1735 series); subfloor / slab underside; pool enclosure gate (see G5).

Recommendation

Engage relevant specialists to verify each item; retain certificates on the property file.

G4
Pool barrier — panel-to-panel & base gap verification required
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140688
Location
Ground Floor — pool fence (pool side)
Group
Pool barrier
Photo references

Gaps between adjacent glass panels, and between the bottom of the panels and the decking, cannot be reliably measured from the photographs.

During the site visit accompanying G3 rectification, measure every panel-to-panel gap and every panel base clearance with a calibrated 100 mm go/no-go gauge. Record and remediate any non-compliant dimensions.

G5
Pool perimeter gate — presence, latch height & self-closing mechanism to confirm
VERIFY
No site photograph
(none — site verification)
Location
Ground Floor — pool perimeter gate
Group
Pool barrier
Photo references
(none — site verification)
Observation

A compliant pool enclosure requires a self-closing, self-latching gate opening outwards from the pool, with latch release mechanism ≥ 1500 mm from finished surface on the outside.

Recommendation

Photograph and measure the pool enclosure gate(s). Verify compliance with all Section 3 requirements of AS 1926.1.

G9
Ground-Floor Bathroom — GPO position near shower / vanity to verify
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140730
Location
Ground Floor — Ground-Floor bathroom
Group
Electrical
Photo references
p1140730, p1140731
Observation

Modern bathroom with frameless glass shower, wall-hung vanity, square floor waste. GPO visible on the wall behind / above the toilet, in close proximity to the glass shower enclosure.

Recommendation

Verify on site that the GPO is outside Zones 0, 1 and 2 (generally ≥ 1.8 m horizontal distance from the shower head outlet). If within Zone 2, confirm IP-rated and RCD-protected. Electrician's certificate to be sighted.

G11
Yellow / amber water in Ground-Floor Bathroom toilet (Observation, not a defect)
OBSERVATION
Primary photo: P1140731
Location
Ground Floor — Ground-Floor bathroom
Group
Plumbing observation
Photo references
p1140731
Observation

Same observation as U11. The condition is addressed dwelling-wide under U11, which correctly identifies the likely cause as inadequate filtration on the recycled rainwater tank supply rather than a plumbing defect.

Recommendation

Known issue — no action required. The yellow / amber colour is attributable to inadequate filtration on the recycled rainwater tank supply. The owner has confirmed that the rainwater tank will not be used, so this observation is recorded for completeness only — no rectification is recommended.

Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 4
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Upper Level
U

Upper Level

Upper-level findings cover the master and guest wet areas, balcony drainage and balustrade compliance, the kitchen gas cooktop commissioning, joinery & flooring expansion provision, and a small set of cosmetic / handover items.

12 findings · 3 Moderate · 6 Minor · 2 Verify · 1 Observation
U12
Guest Suite ventilation + Guest Ensuite shower-recess overflow (Moderate–Major tbc)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140771
Location
Upper Level — Guest Suite / Ensuite
Group
Waterproofing
Photo references
p1140771, p1140773, p1140777, p1140778
Observation

Guest Suite ventilation: main wall of glass is fixed and contributes zero to the ventilation opening area. The only louvre window cannot be opened and is less than 1 m². External face shows uneven render around the louvre frame. A standard residential split-system A/C does not satisfy the NCC natural-ventilation requirement. Guest Ensuite (tested on site): the unenclosed shower recess was run for ~5 minutes; recess has a fall < 15 mm to the waste (does not meet AS 3740) and at the conclusion of testing excess shower water spilled out of the recess onto the main bathroom floor — a functional failure of the wet-area design.

Recommendation

Measure Guest Suite floor area and the openable area of every operable element; if < 5% of floor area, modify the louvre / fixed glazing to provide a compliant openable area, or install a compliant AS 1668.2 mechanical ventilation system. Re-render around the louvre frame to a level finish. Re-grade the shower recess to ≥ 1:80 with a 15 mm step-down or install a retaining screen / hob; re-test with a 5-minute run; reinstate waterproof membrane continuity (AS 3740 certificate required).

U8
Basement enclosed rear-store glass door — ventilation gap (verify)
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140627
Location
Upper Level — basement rear store
Group
Ventilation
Photo references
p1140627
Observation

The glass door enclosing the rear basement store shows a ventilation gap at the perimeter. The adequacy of ventilation for the enclosed store (particularly if mechanical plant or unvented appliances are housed there) needs to be verified.

Recommendation

Measure the gap; confirm the store has a compliant ventilation pathway (natural cross-ventilation or mechanical extraction to AS 1668.2). If the store houses any gas / combustion appliance, confirm combustion-air provisions per AS/NZS 5601.1.

U9
Balcony — no emergency overflow installed; balustrade height / glass grade to verify
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140634
Location
Upper Level — Upper balcony
Group
Drainage & overflow
Photo references
p1140634
Observation

Framed glass balustrade with top rail. Balcony has no emergency overflow installed (no secondary overflow visible to the balcony drainage outlet) — required under AS/NZS 3500.3 where the primary drain can become blocked.

Recommendation

Install a compliant emergency overflow to each balcony drainage point. Site-measure top of handrail to balcony floor to confirm ≥ 1000 mm. Confirm Grade A safety glass; verify panel thickness against AS 1288 tables.

U1
Kitchen gas cooktop — remove packaging & confirm commissioning documentation (reclassified V1 Major → V2 Minor)
MINOR

Location
Upper Level — kitchen — gas cooktop
Group
Gas appliances

p1140652, p1140655, p1140666, p1140683
Observation

On site the cooktop installation is substantially complete. Packaging and loose-knob debris remain to be cleared. Commissioning documentation should be on file before use.

Recommendation

Remove packaging / loose-knob debris and confirm the licensed gasfitter's compliance documentation is on file. Apply a quality penetrating sealer to all natural-stone surfaces (suggested contractor: Glenn — 0433 605 895).

U2

MINOR

Upper Level — kitchen island base
Group
Joinery

Observation

Flexible corrugated waste hose passes through a roughly-cut oversized opening in the cabinet base. Gaps around the hose are unsealed; a second penetration is also visible.

Recommendation

Seal penetrations with a rodent-proof flexible sealant collar. Confirm the dishwasher drain has proper air-break or high-loop installation per AS/NZS 3500.2.

Paint / cornice edge along the skylight frame is uneven and shows patchy setting compound. Small gap visible between the frame and the adjacent ceiling lining in places.

Make good the plasterboard and paint at the skylight perimeter to a Level 4 finish per AS 2589.1.

Excess render / paint visible at the external window and door head transoms — over-painted beads and uneven render edges. A cosmetic defect rather than a weatherline issue.

Cut back the excess render / paint to produce a clean shadow line at each frame head; touch-up paint as required. Verify across all frames in the dwelling.

U10

VERIFY

Waterproofing

Finished wet areas with freestanding bath, natural-stone vanity top, large-format tiled floor, linear channel drain, square floor waste outside shower zone, highlight window above bath. GPOs visible above vanity. Compliance items cannot be verified from photographs alone.

Recommendation

Obtain the AS 3740 waterproofing compliance certificate. Verify GPO clearance from basin (not in Zone 1). Test mechanical exhaust operation. Verify highlight window above bath is Grade A safety glazing.

U3
Window child-safety device — key left in lock (operational)
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140607
Location
Upper Level — Upper bedroom window
Group
Window hardware
Photo references
p1140607
Observation

Key-operated window restrictor device installed on the window frame. Blue key shown inserted in the lock at the time of inspection. Device is installed (compliant) but leaving the key defeats the purpose of the child-safety restrictor.

Recommendation

Remove the key from the lock during normal use and store inaccessible to young children. No physical rectification required.

U11
Yellow / amber water observed in toilet pans — dwelling-wide (Observation, not a defect)
OBSERVATION
Primary photo: P1140588
Location
Upper Level — all toilet pans (dwelling-wide)
Group
Plumbing observation
Photo references
p1140588, p1140613, p1140731
Observation

All toilet pans across the dwelling show water of a distinct yellow / amber colour, consistent between levels. The uniform colouration across all fixtures indicates a supply-side cause rather than fixture-specific staining or an isolated plumbing defect, and is attributable to inadequate filtration on the recycled rainwater tank supply.

Recommendation

Known issue — no action required. The owner has confirmed that the recycled rainwater tank will not be used; the dwelling will operate on mains supply only and this discolouration is not expected to recur. Observation recorded for completeness — no rectification is recommended.

Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 5
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Roof / Exterior
R

Roof / Exterior

The roof and external envelope were inspected from accessible ground / balcony positions only — the roof covering was not accessed (see Disclaimer 2). Findings cover external render, paving junctions, balustrade post-bases, weep-hole drainage and the rooftop A/C condenser.

10 findings · 2 Moderate · 5 Minor · 3 Verify
R3
Partially blocked sample cavity-wall weep hole — clear all weep holes
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140799
Location
Roof / Exterior — external cavity wall
Group
Cavity drainage
Photo references
p1140799, p1140800
Observation

On-site inspection established that the previously-recorded vertical opening at the external wall is in fact a partially-blocked cavity-wall weep hole — not a gap in a separating / party wall. The sample weep hole is partially obstructed by render / paint / debris, which impedes cavity drainage. (Reclassified V1 Major → V2 Moderate.)

Recommendation

Walk every external wall and clear every cavity weep hole — remove render / paint build-up and any debris lodged within. Verify spacing meets AS 3700 (typically not more than 1,200 mm apart at the base of each cavity, immediately above and below openings/lintels). Where weep holes are absent, form additional openings through the external leaf per AS 3700.

R4
Cracks at render-to-paving junctions in multiple entry-paving & stair locations
MODERATE
Primary photo: P1140785
Location
Roof / Exterior — entry paving / stairs
Group
Render
Photo references
p1140785, p1140786
Observation

Visible gaps / cracks at the interface between the base of the rendered walls and the stone paving, running along the wall line in multiple entry-paving and stair locations — direct water ingress paths at horizontal junctions which will progress if not sealed.

Recommendation

Clean out each junction, apply a compressible backer rod, and seal with a neutral-cure silicone sealant suitable for exterior stone / render. Verify the waterproof membrane beneath the paving is continuous against the wall up-stand.

R1
Debris and standing water visible on rooftop deck adjacent to skylights
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140707
Location
Roof / Exterior — upper skylights
Group
Drainage
Photo references
p1140707, p1140708, p1140726
Observation

Skylight glazing with surrounding white aluminium frame set into a stone / paved deck. Organic debris (leaves, twigs, grit) visible on the deck near the skylights. Areas of residual surface water / damp staining visible. Pebble bed in the adjoining roof-planter area contains leaf litter. Pooling water adjacent to a skylight flashing is a known leak entry path.

Recommendation

Clean all debris from rooftop deck and pebble beds. Verify roof falls correctly to nominated drainage point(s) and no ponding exists once cleared. Refer to a roof plumber for flashing and drainage assessment.

R5
Render surface unevenness / bulging revealed by raking light
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140787
Location
Roof / Exterior — rendered façades
Group
Render
Photo references
p1140787, p1140788
Observation

Inspector's torch used at a shallow angle across the render reveals unevenness — a bulge and associated irregularities. Typical raking-light method used to identify imperfect float work or partial delamination of render.

Recommendation

Sound-test flagged areas by tapping. Where hollow, remove loose render back to a sound edge, key the substrate, and re-render per the original specification.

R6
Balustrade post-to-capping junction — corrosion / finish breakdown
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140791
Location
Roof / Exterior — external balustrade post-base
Group
Balustrade
Photo references
p1140791
Observation

Whitish compound residue and what appears to be the beginning of rust staining at the base of one or more balustrade posts where they meet the stone capping. Coating / paint finish shows early breakdown.

Recommendation

Clean the post / capping junction, verify post fixings are sound, strip back flaking coating, treat any corrosion, and recoat with a paint system appropriate to coastal exposure (likely C5-M) per AS/NZS 2312.1. Seal the post-to-capping junction with neutral-cure sealant.

R7
External doors — incomplete finishing & hardware (practical completion)
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140758
Location
Roof / Exterior — external doors (multiple)
Group
External doors
Photo references
p1140758, p1140789
Observation

Unsealed junction between bronze aluminium door frame and rendered wall at the base of the jamb (p1140758). Storage door appears fitted but without a door handle installed (p1140789), only a strike plate visible.

Recommendation

Complete frame-to-render junction with flexible sealant. Install missing door handle / lockset. Verify door is fully weather-sealed (brush / rubber seal at head, jambs and threshold).

R8
Mechanical (Daikin VRV) A/C condenser — foliage obstruction & unfinished render at service penetrations (separate unit from G7) (reclassified V1 Moderate → V2 Minor)
MINOR
Primary photo: P1140797
Location
Roof / Exterior — rooftop A/C condenser
Group
Render
Photo references
p1140797, p1140798
Observation

Large tropical plants (Strelitzia / 'giant bird of paradise') growing directly against the condenser, obstructing airflow to the intake. Service penetration through the render adjacent to the unit — orange conduit (refrigerant line cover) and blue cable visible — render around the penetration not made good.

Recommendation

Cut back or relocate plants to provide clearances per the Daikin installation manual. Engage the builder to make good the render around the service penetration. Engage a licensed HVAC technician and a licensed electrician to verify installation per AS/NZS 3823 and AS/NZS 3000 respectively.

R10
Soffit-to-wall junction at external stair landing — visible crack / junction line
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140789
Location
Roof / Exterior — external stair landing soffit
Group
Stairs & handrails
Photo references
p1140789
Observation

Horizontal line visible along the soffit-to-wall junction. Could be a compliant shadow-line / control-joint detail, or a shrinkage / movement crack.

Recommendation

Inspect the junction on site. If control-joint, dismiss; if a crack, measure width, install a monitor and observe; if active or > 0.3 mm, refer to a structural engineer.

R2
Roof covering / membrane, box gutters, parapet cappings, flashings — not inspected
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140792
Location
Roof / Exterior — roof (above eye level)
Group
Roof (specialist)
Photo references
p1140792, p1140793
Observation

Only limited oblique views of the parapet edge and a single surface-fixed downpipe are visible. The roof surface, waterproofing, box gutters, overflows, rainwater heads, sumps, flashings at parapet cappings, HVAC penetrations, skylights and service vents have not been inspected. From the limited viewpoints available there is no visible evidence of emergency overflows at numerous roof / box-gutter locations.

Recommendation

Engage a licensed roofing contractor and / or roof plumber for a specialist inspection under the roof disclaimer (Appendix B). Particular attention to the presence and sizing of emergency overflows on every enclosed catchment.

R9
Front entry stair — geometry, slip resistance & handrail compliance to verify
VERIFY
Primary photo: P1140786
Location
Roof / Exterior — front entry stair
Group
Stairs & handrails
Photo references
p1140786
Observation

Stair well-presented with LED step lighting. Compliance items that cannot be measured from the photograph: consistent going and riser dimensions; slip resistance of the stone tile nosings under wet conditions; handrail height and continuity; balustrade where fall height > 1 m.

Recommendation

On site, measure rise and going of each tread. Obtain slip-resistance classification or pendulum test per AS 4586. Measure handrail height; confirm balustrade height where fall exceeds 1 m.

Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 6
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Action Plan

Action Plan

Findings grouped by priority tier. Tier 1 items should be addressed before occupancy or during conveyancing.

Tier 1 · Immediate action

Life safety & confirmed NCC non-compliance

Resolve before pool use, cooktop use or occupancy.

  • B7 / G12 — Fire separation basement-to-dwelling absent. Engage a registered Fire Safety Engineer or building certifier; raise under statutory warranty (Home Building Act 1989 NSW); FRL 60/60/60 separating construction + self-closing solid-core doors + AS 1735.11 fire-rated lift landing doors. Install a continuous handrail to the spiral stair (NCC HP 11.2). Interim: no fuel/LPG/lithium-ion in basement; install interconnected smoke alarms; CO monitor at stair head.
  • G3 — Pool barrier excessive lateral flex. Replace with an E1-certified base-mounted glass pool-fence system; verify all AS 1926.1 dimensions; obtain Certificate of Compliance from accredited NSW Pool Barrier Certifier (Phil — 0434 035 119); re-register on NSW Swimming Pool Register.
  • G12B — NW external entry door threshold. Install compliant weather threshold / drip-flashing / storm seal; expansion joint between threshold and floating floor; re-hang door with adequate clearance to remove abrasion damage; make good damaged floorboards.
  • U12 — Guest Ensuite shower-recess overflow. Re-grade the recess to ≥ 1:80 with a 15 mm step-down or install a retaining screen / hob; re-test with a 5-minute run; reinstate AS 3740 waterproof membrane continuity.
  • Emergency overflows (multiple locations). Install compliant emergency overflows to all roof and balcony drainage points (U9, R1, R2); install access traps over the Entertaining-area deck drain(s) and an emergency overflow to the concealed water-catchment tray (G10).
  • U12 — Guest Suite ventilation. Make Guest Suite ventilation compliant with NCC HP H4D5 (5% openable area) or install AS 1668.2 mechanical-ventilation system.
Tier 2 · Address before occupancy or during settlement

Moderate items with meaningful compliance or habitability exposure

  • R3 — Cavity-wall weep holes (Moderate, V2). Clear all partially-blocked weep holes; re-inspect cavity drainage per AS 3700.
  • G1 — Balcony steel post coating. Strip back, prepare substrate, apply AS/NZS 2312.1 paint system (C5-M coastal exposure).
  • G2, G7, R4, R8 — Weatherproofing at frame junctions and service penetrations. Make good render at each location; apply neutral-cure sealant; protect cabling.
  • U5 — Excess render at door / window head. Cut back excess; verify all frames in dwelling.
  • B4, B5, B3A — Basement slab crack; basement door jamb rust + missing under-stair door + drummy render. Measure slab crack; install monitor; remediate corroding element; install missing door leaf; remove and replace drummy render.
  • U8 — Basement rear-store ventilation gap. Verify against NCC HP 10.6 / AS 1668.2.
  • G12A — Floating floor expansion provision. Remove sealant filling at all skirtings; fit micro-bead at Living Room U-angle; install rubber buffers on internal & joinery doors; seal kitchen-island base.
Tier 3 · Verifications & observations

On-site measurement or documentation required

  • U9 — Balcony balustrade height & glass-grade verify (overflow remedy in Tier 1)
  • U10, G9 — Wet-area GPO locations against AS/NZS 3000 Section 6.2 Zones
  • U10 — Waterproofing compliance certificates (AS 3740)
  • G4, G5 — Pool barrier gap measurements; pool-gate compliance (AS 1926.1)
  • G10 — Deck slip-resistance pendulum test (AS 4586) and framing compliance (AS 1684.2)
  • R9, R10 — Front entry stair geometry / slip / handrail; soffit-to-wall junction
  • R2 — Full specialist roof inspection (roofing contractor / roof plumber)
  • U11 / G11 — Yellow water observations (not defects). Known issue — no action required; cause is inadequate filtration on the recycled rainwater tank supply, and the owner has confirmed the rainwater tank will not be used.
Tier 4 · Finishing / cosmetic / handover

Minor items for defect-liability — do not affect compliance

  • U1 — Gas cooktop (Minor in V2). Remove packaging / loose-knob debris; confirm gasfitter's compliance documentation.
  • B1 — Basement retaining wall (Minor in V2). Remove redundant formwork; make good honeycombed concrete; reinstate protective cover.
  • U2 — Seal unsealed cabinet penetrations
  • U3 — Remove child-safety key from lock
  • U4, G8 — Touch-up plasterboard / paint to Level 4 finish
  • U6, U7 — Construction debris & carpet staining — clean
  • R5, R6, R7 — Raking-light render unevenness; balustrade post-base maintenance; missing door handle
  • B3, B6 — Drainage-grate concrete + delaminated paving make-good; garage-door spring replacement
  • G6, R1 — Pool waterline cleaning / commissioning; rooftop debris removal
Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 7
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Appendix

Appendix A — Scope, Methodology & Limits

This inspection was performed on site by Dominic Ogburn on 23 April 2026, on a visual, non-intrusive basis. Findings are based on what was observed during the on-site inspection and recorded in 244 photographs taken during the visit.

Items expressly outside the scope of this inspection

  • Intrusive inspection of concealed elements (membranes behind finishes, reinforcement within concrete)
  • Moisture-meter readings
  • Electrical testing or compliance verification under AS/NZS 3000:2018
  • Lift certification under the AS 1735 series
  • Structural-capacity calculations
  • Thermal / BASIX performance verification

Separate specialist scopes

  • Termite inspection (AS 4349.3 — separate scope)
  • Pool compliance certification (see Disclaimer 1)
  • Roof specialist inspection (see Disclaimer 2)
  • Fire Safety Engineer's written assessment of NCC HP H6D1 compliance
  • NATA-accredited laboratory water testing
  • Pendulum slip-resistance testing (AS 4586:2013)

Standard applied

This inspection has been carried out in the general spirit of AS 4349.1:2007 Inspection of buildings — Pre-purchase inspections — Residential buildings, while explicitly noting the scope limits above. Where a specific standard or NCC provision is engaged by a Major finding the reference appears in that finding.

Note on below-ground waterproofing standards

Australia does not have a dedicated AS Standard for below-ground / tanking membranes equivalent to AS 3740 (internal wet areas) or AS 4654.2 (external above-ground). Below-ground waterproofing is governed by NCC 2022 Volume Two HP 7.5 (Weatherproofing) and HP 3.1.3 (Subsurface drainage), product-specific manufacturer's installation details, and AS 4200.1 / 4200.2 for pliable building membranes.

Access Property Services · 23 April 2026 · Page 8
Access Property Services152 Moverly Road · Appendix B

Appendix B — Scope Disclaimers

Specialist inspections required.

Disclaimer 1 — Pool & pool barrier:

The building inspector is not a licensed pool specialist and this report does not constitute a pool or pool-barrier compliance certificate. Any observations on the pool, pool barrier, coping, water recirculation, or poolside electrical / bonding are indicative only and are subject to inspection and certification by an accredited NSW Pool Barrier Certifier (AS 1926.1 / 1926.2), a licensed pool builder / technician (AS 1926.3), and a licensed electrician for poolside RCD / equipotential bonding (AS/NZS 3000 Section 7.5). The pool must be registered on the NSW Swimming Pool Register with a current Certificate of Compliance before use.

Disclaimer 2 — Roof, roof covering, roof plumbing & concealed roof structure:

The building inspector is not a licensed roofing contractor and this report does not constitute a roof specialist report. The roof covering, box gutters, parapet cappings, flashings, rainwater heads, sumps, overflow provision and concealed roof structure were not inspected from above. Visible roof-edge observations and findings affecting the rooftop deck are provided on an advisory basis only. A full specialist inspection by a licensed roofing contractor and / or roof plumber is required under AS/NZS 3500.3:2021, AS 4654.2:2012 and NCC HP Part 7.5 before any reliance is placed on the roof condition.
